title: Magical Medicinal Turkish Beans Kuru Fasulye
description: A traditional Turkish dish featuring fermented beans, phyllo dough, and shio-cure, known for its potential hangover-curing properties. It is a raw-friendly, glassy-jelly textured dish.

ingredients.name: dried white beans
ingredients.notes: navy or cannellini beans work well

instructions: Soak the dried white beans in water for at least 8 hours or overnight.
Drain and rinse the soaked beans, then transfer them to a large pot with enough water to cover them.
Bring the beans to a boil, then reduce the heat and let simmer for 45 minutes or until they are tender.
While the beans are cooking, prepare the phyllo dough according to package instructions.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat and saute the onion, garlic, carrot, and celery until they are softened.
Add the cooked beans, shio koji, salt, and black pepper to the skillet and stir to combine.
Layer the phyllo dough in a large baking dish, brushing each layer with olive oil.
Add the bean and vegetable mixture to the phyllo dough and fold the dough over to seal.
Bake the kuru fasulye in a preheated oven at 375 degrees for 25 minutes or until the phyllo dough is golden brown.
Let the dish cool before serving, allowing the flavors to meld together.
